deployment.name: Litigation Discovery Triage Agent
deployment.description: Internal AI assistant that triages and summarizes documents during e-discovery. Junior associates upload large doc sets; the agent flags responsive material, drafts privilege-review memos, and produces deposition prep packs. Heavy use of long-context models (Opus / GPT-5.4) because docs commonly run 50-200 pages. Internal-only behind firm VPN.

anchor_query.example: Summarize the relevant communications between executives A and B about the 2024 acquisition discussions. Flag any privilege concerns.

_calibration.notes: Legal-vertical AI deployments with a stable system prompt + responsiveness rubric + corpus injection commonly hit 0.75-0.85 effective cache rate on 7-turn discovery sessions: the system prompt and prior triage decisions persist across turns while only the candidate document varies. 0.80 is a conservative midpoint for AmLaw-grade litigation triage; the prior value (0.68) under-modeled cache reuse for this workload class and inflated the headline ~35%. Operators with measured cache logs should re-fit; the calculator's s-cache slider overrides this value.

doc_triage.description: Read N docs, score responsiveness, summarize

depo_prep.description: Deposition prep pack — long outputs with quoted exhibits

narrow_lookup.description: Targeted Q&A against a single doc

segments.label: Litigation associates
segments.description: Daily users, deep sessions, all authenticated

federal._pii_redaction_note: Re-calibrated 2026-05-12 from 18 → 0.50 /M tokens. The prior value was ~50x industry-typical PII redaction service pricing (AWS Comprehend Detect PII Entities, Azure Cognitive Services PII Detection, Google DLP all run $0.20-1.50 per million characters, roughly $0.05-0.40 per million tokens; $0.50/M tokens is a conservative midpoint covering inline LLM-based PII redaction overhead too). Old value alone added ~$110K/mo of phantom 'federal additive' cost to this preset's headline.
